content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 18,
1992
MS. Rachel Krasney
City Clerk
City of San Bernardino
P.O.
Box 1318
San Bernardino,
CA
92402
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92—320
Dear Ms.
Krasney:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act
(“Act”) •1/
QUESTIONS
1.
An incumbent San Bernardino city council member has two
campaign committees.
One committee is for his city council office
and the other is for his candidacy for the San Bernardino Board of
Supervisors.
Where and when are his controlled committees
required to file campaign disclosure statements?
2.
Where are the campaign statements filed for a committee
called Volunteer Organization to Educate?
ANSWERS
1.
When a campaign statement is due in connection with
either the council office or the supervisorial election, both
committees must file statements with the city clerk and the county
clerk.
2.
Based on the facts you have provided, the actual
activities of the Volunteer Organization to Educate are not clear.
Therefore, please consult the analysis for a general discussion of
where committee statements must be filed.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et .
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Krasney,
Rachael
Our File No.
A-92--320
Page
2
FACTS
In a telephone conversation with me on June 11,
1992,
you
clarified the questions posed in your letter of May 22,
1992.
You
wanted written confirmation of the filing requirements of a San
Bernardino city council member who is also seeking election to the
San Bernardino Board of Supervisors.
Secondly, you asked where a
local ballot measure committee named Volunteer Organization to
Educate established in March,
1992,
should file its campaign
statements.
The Committee’s Statement of Organization states that
it is primarily formed to support San Bernardino city charter
ballot measures and to conduct voter registration in both the city
and county of San Bernardino.
The Committee filed statements with
the county clerk.
However,
you indicated that its activities
between March and June,
1992, appeared only to be in connection
with the city charter measures.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires controlled committees to file campaign
statements at specified times and in specified places which are
determined by the date of the officeholder’s or candidate’s
elections and whether he or she seeks or holds office.
(Sections
84200-84204,
84215.)
In the Barrett Advice Letter
(No.
86-150 and
88—168,
copy enclosed), the Commission advised that committees
controlled by local officeholders who are running for offices in
other jurisdictions and who have two controlled committees must
file campaign statements at all of the times and in all of the
places in which the individuals must file as officeholders and as
candidates.
The purpose of this requirement is to ensure that the
voters in both jurisdictions can be informed about all of the
candidate’s campaign finance activities.
The Act requires that committees formed or existing primarily
to support local measures to be voted upon in a sincTle city must
file campaign statements with the clerk of the city.
Committees
formed primarily to conduct activities which exceed the boundaries
of a city but remain in the jurisdiction of a single county must
file campaign statements with the county clerk.
(Section 84215.)
Without specific facts regarding the actual activities of
Volunteer Organization to Educate, the Commission cannot advise
whether this committee should file with the city clerk or with the
county clerk.
2
,
21
The Commission does not act as a finder of fact in providing
advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
71.)


Krasney,
Rachael
Our File No.
A—92-320
Page
3
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916)
322—5662 if you
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting
neral Cou sel
Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
End
